title: US PPI Forecast Snapshot — July 2026 (Composite Calibrated Bridge Nowcast v2.5 Enhanced)
summary: MEASURED MONTH: JULY 2026 (released Aug 13, 2026 — BLS-verified; NOT the June print due today Jul 15). July PPI forecast: Headline +0.4% MoM, Core +0.3% MoM, provisional YoY ~5.9%/4.7%. This is a mid-month nowcast: July is ~45% sampled, all component inputs provisional, confidence held at 50. The July window is dominated by the US-Iran re-escalation: after the weekend strikes the US reinstated its naval blockade, two UAE tankers were hit by Iranian cruise missiles in Omani waters (one crew dead), and Hormuz transit fell to ~34 ships/day vs the ~88 norm. Brent settled +9.6% on Jul 13 to $83.30 and traded $85.32 on Jul 14; WTI ~$79.79. Crucially, the July PPI sampling week (week of Jul 13) sits ON the spike, so July energy will print up even if a ceasefire lands later in the month. Offset: AAA regular $3.859 and diesel $4.882 (Jul 14) remain below June monthly averages, and Henry Hub $2.89 (-7% MoM) caps the composite. June CPI (out Jul 14) proved passthrough works fast: -0.4% MoM / core 0.0% on the June ceasefire — the mirror image now runs in reverse for July. ISM Prices Paid 73.0 and ECI-anchored services keep core sticky near +0.3%.
baseCase: July 2026 PPI: Headline +0.4% MoM, Core +0.3% MoM. Sampling-week war premium (Brent $85.32, Jul 14) plus the structural distillate crunch (12% below 5-yr avg) lift the July energy composite to ~+3.0% provisional. Core goods +0.4% on ISM 73 passthrough; other services +0.22% via ECI Q1 interpolation. Intermediate-demand uplift +0.10 ppt. YoY drifts toward ~5.9% on the +0.7% July-2025 base effect, pending today's June PPI anchor (consensus 6.2% YoY).

release.name: PPI
release.thesis: July's measurement window captured the re-escalation at the worst possible point: the BLS sampling week (Jul 13-17) opened with Brent +9.6% to $83.30, Iranian cruise-missile strikes on UAE tankers, and Hormuz transit at ~34 ships/day vs the ~88 norm. Because producer gasoline/diesel prices track wholesale spot with a short lag, the July energy composite prints ~+3.0% even though retail pump averages (AAA $3.859/$4.882) still trail June monthly means — and even if diplomacy reverses crude in the second half. Core stays sticky near +0.3% via ISM Prices Paid 73.0 and ECI-anchored services; June CPI's 0.0% core proved goods passthrough is muted, which caps core upside. Base effect (+0.7% in July 2025) pulls YoY toward ~5.9%. Mid-month nowcast: ~55% of the window unsampled; today's June PPI (08:30 ET, consensus 6.2% YoY) resets the prior-month anchor.

scenarios.label: Base Case — Sampling Week Locks the Spike
scenarios.outcome: July headline PPI +0.4% MoM, core +0.3% MoM, YoY ~5.9%/4.7%. Energy composite ~+3.0% as the Jul 13-17 sampling week embeds the $83-85 Brent spike and the distillate crunch; natgas (-7% MoM) and sub-June retail pump averages cap the upside. Core sticky via ISM 73 / ECI.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Mildly hawkish: September hike odds (~60% after soft June CPI) reprice higher, 10Y back toward 4.60-4.65%. YoY dipping under 6% on base effects gives doves a talking point, but MoM re-acceleration keeps the Fed's hawkish split alive into the Jul 28-29 FOMC.
scenarios.invalidation: Signed ceasefire before the Jul 13-17 sampling week closes AND WTI weekly close back under $72; EIA shows product inventories normalizing

scenarios.label: Hot Case — Sustained Hormuz Disruption
scenarios.outcome: July headline PPI +0.8% MoM, core +0.5% MoM. Tanker strikes escalate into sustained transit throttling through the full month; Brent holds $90+; diesel/jet crack spreads blow out on the 12%-below-average distillate stock base; energy composite +6-8%; second-round pass into core goods and freight.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Full stagflation repricing: 2Y Treasury reverses the post-CPI rally back above 4.30%, September hike odds >75%, hike-at-July-FOMC talk emerges, USD firms through DXY 102, equities de-rate. July CPI (Aug 12) one day before PPI amplifies the stack.
scenarios.invalidation: Diplomatic resolution plus OPEC+ supply response; Brent back under $75 for two consecutive weeks; EIA distillate stocks rebuild above 110M bbl

scenarios.label: Cool Case — Ceasefire Round-Trip
scenarios.outcome: July headline PPI 0.0% MoM, core +0.1% MoM. Ceasefire re-signed within days; crude round-trips to $70 by month-end; monthly-average energy composite flips negative (~-2%) as natgas (-7% MoM) and still-falling retail fuel averages dominate; core goods cool with DXY ~100.7 and soft import prices.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Inflation scare unwinds a second time: September hike pricing fades below 40%, 10Y toward 4.40%, duration rallies, growth equities recover. YoY slides toward 5.6-5.7% on the base effect, echoing June CPI's -0.4% print.
scenarios.invalidation: Any kinetic escalation during the remaining July window; distillate crunch triggers a diesel spike regardless of crude direction

setups.bias: Long through the July sampling window while Hormuz risk is live and crack spreads widen
setups.trigger: Hormuz transit stays below ~50 vessels/day; Brent holds $83+; EIA distillate stocks stay 10%+ below the 5-yr avg in today's w/e Jul 10 report
setups.invalidation: Signed ceasefire + Brent weekly close below $75

setups.bias: Long as stagflation/geopolitical hedge into the CPI+PPI double-header (Aug 12-13)
setups.trigger: Hot July CPI/PPI stack with continued escalation; real yields capped by growth scare even as 10Y backs up
setups.invalidation: Cool prints + ceasefire: real yields pop, gold extends its Jul 13-14 slide below $4,000

setups.bias: Tactical short on hot-case confirmation; long only on a cool CPI+PPI stack
setups.trigger: July core PPI > +0.4% with a hot July CPI — sell duration as Sept hike odds reprice above 75%
setups.invalidation: Cool stack + ceasefire flips to growth-scare pricing — cover and go long

setups.bias: Long on hot July prints with rates follow-through
setups.trigger: Headline + core both firm; DXY reclaims 102 from the current 100.73
setups.invalidation: Cool prints + ceasefire; DXY back under 100

watchItems.label: MEASURED MONTH = JULY 2026
watchItems.value: Release Aug 13, 2026, 08:30 ET — VERIFIED on the official BLS PPI schedule (bls.gov/schedule/news_release/ppi.htm)
watchItems.note: This file forecasts July price changes. The June PPI (today, Jul 15, 08:30 ET) is a separate earlier release and only enters here as the prior-month anchor.

watchItems.label: PROVISIONAL — July sampling incomplete
watchItems.note: All composite inputs are July-to-date through the Jul 14 close. Second-half July data (especially energy, post any ceasefire) can swing the energy composite by ±3 ppt.

watchItems.label: US-Iran / Hormuz escalation
watchItems.value: Brent $85.32, WTI ~$79.79 (Jul 14); Brent settled +9.6% Jul 13; 2 UAE tankers hit by Iranian cruise missiles; ~34 vs ~88 ships/day
watchItems.note: Dominant July input. US naval blockade reinstated; CENTCOM says the strait is open but traffic collapsed. Resolution timing decides base vs hot vs cool.

watchItems.label: June PPI actual — prints TODAY
watchItems.value: Jul 15, 08:30 ET; consensus 6.2% YoY (vs 6.5% May); our June nowcast was +0.1% MoM
watchItems.note: Not yet released at run time. Becomes the prior-month anchor for July YoY math; May revisions possible. Current prior fields = May actuals (+1.1%/6.5%, core +0.4%/4.9%).

watchItems.label: EIA WPSR w/e Jul 10 — today 10:30 ET
watchItems.value: Survey: crude -2.7M, gasoline/distillate builds; prior wk: distillate -5.0M to 103.6M (12% below 5-yr avg), SPR -3M to 316.5M (lowest since Apr 1983)
watchItems.note: Confirms whether the fuel-supply crunch is deepening into the July sampling window.

watchItems.label: June CPI actual (Jul 14) — cooler
watchItems.value: Headline -0.4% MoM / 3.5% YoY; core 0.0% MoM / 2.6% YoY (below 3.9%/2.8% forecasts)
watchItems.note: June's ceasefire relief passed through fast — evidence that energy swings hit the indexes within one month, which is how the July re-spike enters July PPI. Soft core (0.0%) caps core-goods passthrough assumptions.

watchItems.label: BLS ECI Q2 2026 (Jul 31)
watchItems.note: Lands inside the July measurement window — Q2 >1.0% lifts the July services composite.

watchItems.label: ISM Manufacturing (Jul data, ~Aug 1-3)
watchItems.note: July ISM Prices Paid is the last major core-goods input; >70 keeps the hot case alive.

watchItems.label: VIX regime
watchItems.note: Normal regime (<18): 3-month bias window, 1-month energy/core-goods lag, 1.96 CI multiplier. A close above 18 flips the model to Elevated.

keyDrivers: Sampling-week energy spike: the BLS July pricing week (Jul 13-17) opened with Brent settling +9.6% to $83.30 and trading $85.32, WTI +9.4% to $78.14, after the US reinstated its Hormuz blockade and Iranian missiles hit two UAE tankers. Producer fuel prices track wholesale spot with a short lag, so July energy prints ~+3.0% even though retail averages (AAA $3.859 gas / $4.882 diesel) remain below June monthly means.

Distillate crunch is structural, not just geopolitical: stocks 12% below the 5-yr average at 103.6M bbl, SPR at its lowest since April 1983, Russia's diesel export ban still on — diesel/jet stay bid even if crude cools, and today's EIA w/e Jul 10 report tests whether the crunch deepened.

Core stickiness with a lower ceiling: ISM Prices Paid 73.0 (+2.3 ppt normalized) and ECI Q1 +0.9% SA keep July core near +0.3%, but June CPI's 0.0% core MoM shows goods passthrough is muted — capping core upside versus the May +0.4% pace.

Base effect cuts YoY: July 2025 PPI was +0.7% MoM, so a +0.4% July 2026 print mechanically pulls headline YoY down ~0.3 ppt from the June anchor (consensus 6.2%, printing today) toward ~5.9% — MoM re-acceleration hidden inside a softer YoY.
Natural gas remains the offset: Henry Hub $2.89 (-7% MoM, storage +6.6% above the 5-yr avg, Freeport LNG maintenance) keeps the July energy composite from running hotter despite the heat dome.
risks: Mid-month provisional risk: ~55% of the July measurement window is unsampled. A ceasefire this week converts the base case toward the cool case (headline ~0.0%) — the same round-trip June just delivered.

Upside — sustained disruption: if tanker strikes continue and Brent holds $90+ into early August, the hot case (+0.8% headline, +6-8% energy) becomes the base; diesel cracks amplify it via the inventory crunch.

Anchor risk: today's June PPI (08:30 ET, consensus 6.2% YoY) and its May revisions reset the prior-month base used in July YoY math; a big June surprise shifts the July YoY estimate by up to ±0.3 ppt.

Sampling-timing asymmetry: with the spike sitting ON the Jul 13-17 sampling week, even a late-July diplomatic resolution cannot fully reverse the July print — the cool case needs crude lower within days, not weeks.
Regime risk: VIX 16.40 keeps the model in Normal, but Aug VIX futures at 18.80 signal the market expects escalation; a spot close above 18 widens the CI and shifts the bias window.
